title: Jacobs Solutions Inc. (J) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Jacobs Solutions Inc. (J) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 6.5% FCF growth rate assumes steady, single-digit revenue growth driven by infrastructure and advanced facilities demand, combined with slight margin expansion as the business mix shifts toward higher-value consulting services post-spin-off.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate reflects the company's moderate cyclicality, offset by a strong balance sheet and predictable cash flows from long-term consulting and engineering contracts.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term macroeconomic growth expectations, reflecting the ongoing, fundamental need for infrastructure upgrade and maintenance globally.

thesisTitle: A Focused Infrastructure and Sustainability Play
thesisParagraphs: Following the successful separation of its Critical Mission Solutions and Cyber & Intelligence businesses, Jacobs Solutions has emerged as a leaner, more focused organization. This strategic move eliminates the conglomerate discount that previously weighed on the stock and allows management to concentrate fully on the higher-margin, higher-growth areas of critical infrastructure, water, and advanced facilities design. By shedding lower-margin government IT contracts, the core business now boasts a more attractive financial profile characterized by strong free cash flow generation and improved visibility.

The newly streamlined Jacobs is exceptionally well-positioned to capitalize on several massive, multi-decade secular trends. The urgent need to upgrade aging infrastructure, the global push for water security and sustainability, and the massive investments in domestic manufacturing capabilities (particularly in semiconductors and life sciences) provide a robust, long-term tailwind. The current market price does not fully reflect the enhanced margin potential and durable competitive advantages of this focused infrastructure pure-play.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: How does the Amentum spin-off affect the valuation?
faq.answer: The spin-off removes lower-margin, slower-growth segments, leaving a core business with structurally higher margins and better growth prospects. Our valuation model focuses solely on the cash flow generation of the remaining infrastructure and advanced facilities business.

faq.question: What is driving the 6.5% growth assumption?
faq.answer: The growth assumption is underpinned by the massive global need for infrastructure renewal and the reshoring of complex manufacturing capabilities, areas where Jacobs holds leading market positions and deep technical expertise.

faq.question: Is Jacobs vulnerable to a recession?
faq.answer: While cyclical, a significant portion of Jacobs' revenue is tied to long-term infrastructure projects and non-discretionary spending (like water infrastructure), providing a degree of resilience even during economic downturns.
